title: Model with Arrays
essential_question: How can you use arrays to solve division problems?
strategies: Place tiles in rows of the given number, count rows.
Or place tiles in the given number of rows, count columns.
Write division and multiplication equations for the array.

pretest.question: An array has 3 rows of 8 tiles. How many tiles in all?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.6 Lesson 6.6 'Model with Arrays' pp.241-244

learn.title: Arrays and Division
learn.content: An array is objects arranged in equal rows and columns. You can use arrays to show division. 24 tiles in 4 rows means 24 ÷ 4 = 6 tiles per row.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.6 Lesson 6.6 'Model with Arrays' pp.241-244
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D — Arrays for Multiplication and Division
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Building an Array
learn.content: To solve 18 ÷ 3: make 3 rows with 18 tiles. Keep adding tiles evenly to each row. Each row gets 6 tiles. So 18 ÷ 3 = 6.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.6 Lesson 6.6 'Model with Arrays' pp.241-244
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D — Arrays for Multiplication and Division
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: One Array, Two Equations
learn.content: The same array shows both multiplication and division: 4 × 6 = 24 and 24 ÷ 4 = 6. Flip it: 6 × 4 = 24 and 24 ÷ 6 = 4. They are related facts.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.6 Lesson 6.6 'Model with Arrays' pp.241-244
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D — Arrays for Multiplication and Division
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Reading Division from an Array Diagram
learn.content: Look at a 5 × 7 array (5 rows, 7 columns = 35 tiles). You can read two division facts: 35 ÷ 5 = 7 (find columns) and 35 ÷ 7 = 5 (find rows). The array picture makes both facts visible at once.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.6 Lesson 6.6 'Model with Arrays' pp.241-244
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D — Arrays for Multiplication and Division
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Division from Arrays: The Rule
learn.content: For any array: rows × columns = total. So: total ÷ rows = columns, and total ÷ columns = rows. To find a missing side, divide the total by the side you know. Example: 6 × ? = 54 → 54 ÷ 6 = 9.

visual_data.formula: rows × cols = total  →  total ÷ rows = cols

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.6 Lesson 6.6 'Model with Arrays' pp.241-244
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D — Arrays for Multiplication and Division
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 배열로 나눗셈 풀기 — 3단계 루틴
learn.content: 배열은 행과 열로 정렬된 직사각형 모양의 점/타일. 단계 1) 전체 개수와 한 차원(행 수 또는 열 수) 확인. 단계 2) 식 세우기: 전체 ÷ 알고있는차원 = 모르는차원.   예: 24개를 4행으로 → 24 ÷ 4 = 6 → 한 행에 6개. 단계 3) 곱셈으로 검산: 4 × 6 = 24 ✓. 흔한 실수 (M02): '몇 행' 묻는데 '한 행에 몇 개'라고 답.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.6 Lesson 6.6 'Model with Arrays' pp.241-244
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D — Arrays for Multiplication and Division
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 하나의 배열 = 4개의 식 — 패밀리 만들기
learn.content: 한 배열(예: 4행 × 6열 = 24개)로 4가지 식을 모두 쓸 수 있음: 곱셈 2개: 4 × 6 = 24, 6 × 4 = 24. 나눗셈 2개: 24 ÷ 4 = 6, 24 ÷ 6 = 4. 이를 '패밀리'라 부름. 한 배열을 보면 4식이 즉시 보여야 함. 팁: 곱셈을 알면 나눗셈도 자동으로 알 수 있음 (역연산).

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.6 Lesson 6.6 'Model with Arrays' pp.241-244
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D — Arrays for Multiplication and Division
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 차원 검산 — 행 수 × 열 수 = 전체
learn.content: 배열 답을 적은 뒤 검산: 행 수 × 열 수 = 위에서 본 전체? 예: 7행 × 8열 → 56? 7 × 8 = 56 ✓. 예: 9행 × 4열 → 36? 9 × 4 = 36 ✓. 🔍 검증: 차원 두 개를 곱해서 전체와 일치해야 함. 흔한 실수 (M02): 차원을 헷갈려 답 단위가 틀림 — '행'을 묻는지 '열'을 묻는지 항상 확인.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.6 Lesson 6.6 'Model with Arrays' pp.241-244
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D — Arrays for Multiplication and Division
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

try.explanation: 36 ÷ 4 = 9. The array has 4 rows, so each row has 36 ÷ 4 = 9 tiles.
hints: The array is 4 rows × 9 columns = 36 tiles. Divide by the number of rows.

feedback.correct: Yes! 36 ÷ 4 = 9. Each of the 4 rows has 9 tiles.

try.question: An array has 4 rows and 9 columns. What is 36 ÷ 4?
try.feedback_correct: Yes! 36 ÷ 4 = 9. Each of the 4 rows has 9 tiles.

practice_r2.question: Find the difference: 706 − 348.
practice_r2.explanation: 706−348: 6−8 borrow → 16−8=8; tens 9−4=5 (after lending across zero); hundreds 6−3=3. Result: 358.

hints: A square array has rows = columns. Find a number × itself = 81.

feedback.correct: Correct! 9 × 9 = 81, so the array has 9 rows.
feedback.incorrect: Not quite. 9 × 9 = 81. The array is a 9 × 9 square.

practice_r3.feedback_correct: Correct! 9 × 9 = 81, so the array has 9 rows.

practice_r3.explanation: 6 rows: 48 ÷ 6 = 8 per row. 8 rows: 48 ÷ 8 = 6 per row. 8 − 6 = 2 more.
hints: Find tiles per row for each case: 48 ÷ 6 = ? and 48 ÷ 8 = ?

feedback.correct: Correct! 48 ÷ 6 = 8 and 48 ÷ 8 = 6, so 2 more tiles per row.

practice_r3.question: Kim makes an array with 48 tiles. She can make it with 6 rows or 8 rows. When she uses 6 rows, how many MORE tiles per row compared to 8 rows?
practice_r3.feedback_correct: Correct! 48 ÷ 6 = 8 and 48 ÷ 8 = 6, so 2 more tiles per row.

hints: Find original rows: 48 ÷ 6 = 8 rows. Add 2 rows of 6 each.
